message:
feat(core): add unix username detection for agent name mistakes

Add looks_like_unix_username() that detects lowercase alphanumeric
strings (2-16 chars) that aren't known adjectives or nouns. Wired into
detect_agent_name_mistake() as category 6 (UNIX_USERNAME_AS_AGENT).

Error message matches Python character-for-character including the
resource://agents/{project_key} guidance.

7 new tests for unix username detection, 50 total model tests passing.

Closes: br-3oyfw (T3.3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"br-2cph","title":"T8.2: Implement native HTTP probe module with timeout/retry/error-capture semantics","description":"## Objective
Implement native HTTP probing utilities for deployment validation without `curl` shell dependency.

## Work
- Build probe client with configurable timeout, retry, and redirect policy.
- Capture status code, relevant headers, body snippets, and transport errors.
- Support deterministic request sequencing for reproducibility.

## Constraints
- Must be cross-platform.
- Must produce structured failure reasons suitable for CI artifacting.

## Deliverable
Reusable probe module consumed by verify-live command.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:40.657083912Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:19:19.590785618Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:19:10.267996740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["deploy","http","share"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2cph","depends_on_id":"br-y3sk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:53.188757503Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":367,"issue_id":"br-2cph","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Delivered: probe.rs module (665 lines, 26 tests). Sync HTTP/1.1 client with: URL parsing (http/https, ports, IPv6), configurable timeout/retry/redirect, structured ProbeError (InvalidUrl/DnsError/TlsError/Timeout/etc), ProbeResponse with headers/body/status/redirects, chunked transfer-encoding, HTTPS via curl subprocess (no unsafe dependency), multi-probe runner (run_probe_checks). All 224 share tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-12T05:19:19Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-2dg8","title":"guard is_expired() uses inconsistent boundary: <= for RFC3339 but < for NaiveDateTime","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-09T17:53:51.300427133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T17:54:35.720608921Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T17:54:35.720590757Z","close_reason":"Changed NaiveDateTime branches to use <= instead of < to match RFC3339 branch and DB layer semantics","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-2di","title":"CLI: share preview parity (hotkeys + exit codes)","description":"## Objective
Implement `share preview` command with legacy server behavior and hotkeys.

## Scope
- Defaults: host `127.0.0.1`, port `9000`, `--open-browser` default false.
- Serve static bundle with no-cache headers.
- Hotkeys: `r` reload, `d` deploy request, `q` quit.
- Exit code **42** when deploy requested via `d`.

## Tests
- Integration test with temp bundle; verify server responds and hotkeys trigger.
- Assert exit code 42 on deploy request.

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store preview server logs under `tests/artifacts/cli/share_preview/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. share preview matches legacy defaults and hotkey behavior.
2. Exit codes match legacy (42 on deploy request).
3. No-cache headers are set on responses.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralDog","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:04.316431054Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:50:39.558932277Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:50:39.558909405Z","close_reason":"Implemented share preview server parity: /__preview__/status + no-cache headers, hotkeys r/d/q with exit code 42 on deploy; added integration test + crossterm","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2di","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:44.188268904Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-2di","depends_on_id":"br-2ei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:17:08.106147106Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-2e9jp","title":"TUI Revamp: FrankenTUI Integration & WASM Web Mode","description":"# TUI Revamp: FrankenTUI Integration & WASM Web Mode

## Overview

This epic covers a comprehensive overhaul of the MCP Agent Mail TUI to leverage FrankenTUI's advanced capabilities and add a browser-based dashboard via WASM compilation.

## Goals

1. **Performance**: Sub-16ms frame times even with 1000+ agents
2. **Visual Polish**: Smooth animations, thoughtful theming, accessibility
3. **User Experience**: Intuitive navigation, powerful command palette, toast feedback
4. **Web Access**: Browser-based dashboard for remote monitoring via WASM

## Background & Rationale

The current TUI has several pain points identified through usage analysis:
- Monolithic MailAppModel (>3000 LOC) with tangled state
- Linear list scanning causing O(n) scroll performance
- Hardcoded colors lacking accessibility support
- No animation system for visual feedback
- No web-based access for remote monitoring

FrankenTUI provides solutions for all these issues:
- VirtualizedList with Fenwick Tree for O(log n) scroll
- Damped spring animation system
- Semantic color slots with WCAG compliance
- ftui-web for WASM browser deployment

## Success Criteria

- All existing TUI functionality preserved
- Frame time p95 < 16ms with 10K message list
- WCAG AA color contrast compliance
- WASM build size < 2MB gzipped
- 90%+ test coverage for new components
- Zero accessibility regressions","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:45:50.075539652Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T04:35:14.972699522Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-2e9jp.1","title":"Phase 1: Architecture Foundation - Refactor for Performance","description":"# Phase 1: Architecture Foundation - Refactor for Performance

## Objective
Restructure the TUI architecture to support high-performance rendering and enable FrankenTUI integration.

## Current State Analysis

The existing MailAppModel in crates/mcp-agent-mail-tui/src/model.rs has grown organically to >3000 LOC with:
- Screen state, navigation, and rendering logic interleaved
- Direct database queries scattered throughout update handlers
- No caching layer leading to redundant queries
- Three separate rendering paths (TUI, headless, JSON) with duplicated logic

## Deliverables

- T1.1: ScreenManager Extraction - Dedicated manager owning screen lifecycle
- T1.2: Lazy Screen Rendering - Screens only load data when first visited
- T1.3: Query Batcher - Coalesce DB operations within 8ms batches
- T1.4: Unified Output Path - Single pipeline for TUI, headless, and JSON modes
- T1.5: State Bridge - Reactive state with automatic cache invalidation
- T1.6: Focus Manager - Centralized keyboard focus tracking

## Performance Targets

- Startup time: ~800ms -> <200ms
- Screen switch: ~150ms -> <16ms  
- Scroll (10K items): ~50ms/frame -> <8ms/frame
- DB queries/frame: 5-15 -> 1-2","notes":"Phase 1 completion rollup: child tasks br-2e9jp.1.1/.1.2/.1.3/.1.4/.1.5 are all closed with validation evidence. Phase 1 architecture foundation is complete and downstream blockers can be cleared.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RoseCave","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:46:05.522695320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T03:27:11.481894639Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T03:27:11.481874541Z","close_reason":"All phase-1 child deliverables (T1.1–T1.6) are now closed with implementation/test evidence in child notes; closing parent rollup to reflect completion.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-2e9jp.1","depends_on_id":"br-2e9jp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T22:46:05.522695320Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3ok6s","title":"T5.1: Implement TreeItem adapter for thread messages","description":"Create a TreeItem implementation that wraps thread message data for the frankentui Tree widget.

TREE ITEM STRUCTURE:
```rust
pub struct ThreadTreeItem {
    pub message_id: i64,
    pub sender: String,
    pub subject_snippet: String,
    pub relative_time: String,
    pub is_unread: bool,
    pub is_ack_required: bool,
    pub children: Vec<ThreadTreeItem>,
}

impl TreeItem for ThreadTreeItem {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, is_selected: bool, is_expanded: bool) {
        // Render: "▶ GoldHawk: Start implementation [2m ago]"
        // With unread = bold, ack_required = badge, selected = highlight
    }
    fn children(&self) -> &[Self] { &self.children }
}
```

Build the tree from flat message list:
- Messages with `reply_to_id` become children of the referenced message
- Messages without `reply_to_id` are root nodes
- Sort children by timestamp ascending

FILES: tui_widgets.rs, tui_screens/threads.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] TreeItem renders sender, snippet, time, badges
- [ ] Unread messages render bold
- [ ] Ack-required messages show badge
- [ ] Tree builds correctly from flat message list via reply_to_id
- [ ] Root messages sort chronologically
- [ ] Unit tests with nested reply chains

- [ ] Detailed diagnostics artifacts are produced (scenario IDs, timing breakdowns, failure reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","notes":"Validated TreeItem adapter and reply-chain tree construction are implemented in tui_widgets.rs + tui_screens/threads.rs with unit coverage (nested chain ordering, root chronological ordering, orphan-parent fallback).  {"id":"br-3op","title":"Share: bundle attachments (inline/detach/external/missing)","description":"## Objective
Implement share pipeline Step 8: attachment bundling and classification.

## Scope
- Thresholds: inline <= 64KiB, detach >= 25MiB (defaults, override via CLI).
- Classify attachments into inline (embed), detached (file), external (URL), missing.
- Generate attachment manifest entries with hashes and metadata.
- Honor `convert_images` and attachment embed policy.

## Tests
- Unit tests for threshold logic and classification.
- Integration tests with sample attachments (inline, large, external, missing).

## Logging/Artifacts
- Store attachment manifests and hashes under `tests/artifacts/share/attachments/<timestamp>/`.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Attachment classification matches legacy thresholds and rules.
2. Manifests include correct hashes and metadata.
3. Missing/external attachments are handled gracefully with clear markers.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:15:56.123691848Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:56.435100382Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:00:56.435066689Z","close_reason":"Attachment bundling/classification already implemented in bundle.rs; bundle_* tests pass","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3op","depends_on_id":"br-1uf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T16:16:02.590128847Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"br-3p7th","title":"R4.2: Implement am robot metrics — tool performance summary (calls, errors, error%, latency percentiles)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:17:18.912768416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T05:16:40.375602663Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T05:16:40.375582285Z","close_reason":"Implemented: R4.2 robot metrics with per-tool calls/errors/latency, alert generation for high error rates and slow tools","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-3p7th","depends_on_id":"br-1nmyh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T02:20:54.114144327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":314,"issue_id":"br-3p7th","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# R4.2: `am robot metrics`

## What
Tool performance summary from the query tracking system. Shows agents which tools are slow, erroring, or heavily used. Uses the existing `tool_metrics_snapshot_full()` function that powers the TUI Tool Metrics screen.

## Data Source
Call `tool_metrics_snapshot_full()` which returns per-tool metrics:
- call_count, error_count, avg_latency_ms, p95_ms, p99_ms
- These are computed from the global QueryTracker singleton

## Output Format
```
summary:
  total_calls: 1234
  total_errors: 12
  error_rate: 0.97%
  avg_latency_ms: 45

tools[10]{name,calls,errors,error_pct,avg_ms,p95_ms,p99_ms}:
  fetch_inbox,450,0,0.0,12,25,45
  send_message,200,3,1.5,89,150,200
  search_messages,180,1,0.6,120,250,400
  file_reservation_paths,150,0,0.0,8,15,20
  acknowledge_message,100,0,0.0,5,10,15
  register_agent,50,2,4.0,30,50,80
  health_check,40,0,0.0,3,5,8
  summarize_thread,30,5,16.7,2500,4000,5000
  macro_start_session,20,1,5.0,150,300,500
  whois,14,0,0.0,6,10,15

_alerts[1]{severity,summary}:
  warn,summarize_thread has 16.7% error rate (5/30 calls)
```

## Alert Generation
- Tool with error_pct > 10% → warn alert
- Tool with avg_ms > 2000 → warn alert (slow tool)
- Tool with error_pct > 50% → error alert
- Overall error_rate > 5% → error alert

## Sorting
Default: sort by call count descending (most-used first).
Can be changed to sort by error rate or latency via --sort flag (future enhancement).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Summary section aggregates totals correctly
- Per-tool metrics match actual tracker data
- Alert thresholds trigger correctly
- Percentile calculation correct (matches existing p95/p99 logic)
- Works when metrics are empty (no tools called yet)

Finalize cutover by removing Python fallback path usage and documenting migration intent for future maintainers.

## Work
- Remove or gate legacy script-resolution code paths in CLI.
- Add explicit deprecation messaging where compatibility stubs remain.
- Update docs/runbooks to point to native wizard behavior.
- Record migration rationale and risk notes in bead comments.

## Deliverable
No operational dependency on `scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` for wizard command behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.

-{"id":"br-ws0tf","title":"T2.3: Add inline image rendering with terminal protocol auto-detection","description":"When message bodies contain image attachments or inline image references, render them directly
in the terminal using the best available image protocol.

FRANKENTUI IMAGE SUPPORT:
- ftui_extras provides image rendering via multiple protocols
- Protocol detection order: Kitty > iTerm2 > Sixel > ASCII fallback
- Auto-detection queries terminal capabilities at startup

INTEGRATION:
- During markdown rendering, when encountering an image reference:
  1. Check if image data is available (from attachment pipeline)
  2. Detect terminal image protocol capability
  3. Render inline if protocol available
  4. Show placeholder `[Image: filename.png]` if not
- Image rendering respects the available area (scale to fit)
- WebP images from the attachment pipeline are already optimized

RISK: Not all terminals support image protocols. The fallback path must be robust.
Testing in CI requires protocol mocking.

FILES: tui_screens/messages.rs, tui_widgets.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Images render inline in Kitty terminals
- [ ] Images render inline in iTerm2 terminals
- [ ] Sixel fallback for compatible terminals
- [ ] ASCII art placeholder for unsupported terminals
- [ ] Images scale to fit available preview area
- [ ] No crash if image data unavailable

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"OrangeRobin","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:21:07.821919737Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["images","rendering","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-ws0tf","depends_on_id":"br-127ka","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:31.022539704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"br-ws0tf","depends_on_id":"br-2jn7d","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:08:09.497163361Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Added build_inline_image_block_with_hints() for deterministic protocol tests, dispatch for Kitty/iTerm2/Sixel/Ascii, payload preparation via ftui_extras::image encode_kitty/encode_iterm2, and robust ASCII fallback/no-crash handling for missing or undecodable images. Added tests: collect_markdown_image_refs_parses_alt_and_source, build_inline_image_block_kitty_path_uses_protocol_dispatch, build_inline_image_block_iterm2_path_uses_protocol_dispatch, build_inline_image_block_sixel_path_uses_ascii_fallback_note, build_inline_image_block_handles_missing_file_without_panicking, plus existing render_detail/json and markdown timing diagnostics validated.  {"id":"br-x8a0","title":"T7.2: Define typed wizard domain model + JSON output schema for native Rust implementation","description":"## Objective
Define a typed Rust domain model for the native share wizard so provider checks, plan generation, and CLI output are deterministic and testable.

## Work
- Introduce data model for wizard inputs, detected environment, recommended provider path, and execution plan.
- Define stable JSON schema for `--json` mode.
- Define error taxonomy (validation vs environment vs execution errors).
- Specify explicit exit code contract aligned with existing CLI conventions.

## Deliverable
Rust model/spec ready for implementation and tests, with serialization contracts.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Defines explicit inputs, outputs, invariants, error taxonomy, and exit-code behavior for downstream implementation.
- Captures parity mapping to legacy behavior, including intentional deviations and technical rationale.
- Includes a concrete validation strategy (unit + integration + e2e) and detailed logging/artifact expectations for downstream tasks.
- Is self-contained and executable without referring back to external markdown plan documents.
- Leaves no ambiguous requirements that would permit incompatible implementations.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:00.524485167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T07:46:38.328430268Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T07:46:38.328411243Z","close_reason":"Completed: wizard.rs domain model with HostingProvider, WizardInputs, DetectedEnvironment, DeploymentPlan, WizardResult structs, WizardErrorCode taxonomy (17 codes), exit_codes module, WizardJsonOutput. 5 passing tests.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["design","share","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-x8a0","depends_on_id":"br-sk3y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:12.519843289Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-xe0b","title":"T7.5: Build native interactive prompt flow with validation and non-interactive safeguards","description":"## Objective
Implement an interactive TTY wizard UX in Rust that supersedes Python prompts while preserving operator clarity.

## Work
- Build prompt flow for provider selection, path decisions, and confirmation steps.
- Enforce robust input validation and re-prompt behavior.
- Add non-interactive safeguards: fail fast when required parameters missing.
- Keep UX concise and actionable for CI and local ops usage.

## Deliverable
Interactive flow integrated with plan engine and ready for CLI command wiring.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation is native Rust and cross-platform, introducing no new operational dependency on external shell/Python tooling.
- Preserves required legacy behavior (or documented intentional changes) with deterministic CLI/API semantics and stable `--json` output where applicable.
- Emits structured diagnostics and runtime telemetry sufficient for user-facing troubleshooting and CI artifact capture.
- Has comprehensive test coverage tied to this behavior (unit + integration + e2e where relevant), including negative-path assertions.
- Demonstrates reproducible behavior under CI constraints (timeouts, retries, deterministic ordering/seed controls).
- Produces explicit logging output suitable for rapid root-cause analysis when failures occur.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:01.165508451Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T08:22:32.452751762Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T08:22:32.452732425Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["share","ux","wizard"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-xe0b","depends_on_id":"br-3rf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:45:13.440172694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"br-xqs2q","title":"T1: Search cluster tool description parity","description":"Copy the EXACT Python docstring for each tool in the Search cluster into the Rust #[tool(description = "...")] attribute.

Tools to update: search_messages, summarize_thread

For each tool:
1. Read the Python docstring from /dp/mcp_agent_mail/src/mcp_agent_mail/app.py
2. Copy it character-for-character into the Rust tool description
3. Verify the parameter names, types, and descriptions match
4. Ensure any example JSON-RPC calls are preserved

The fastmcp_rust framework passes tool descriptions through to MCP clients as-is,
so the full multi-paragraph docstring should be used.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-15T01:58:44.806165120Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-15T03:22:25.185118280Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T03:22:25.185099154Z","close_reason":"Search cluster (search_messages, search_messages_product, summarize_thread, summarize_thread_product) descriptions match Python. 34/34 parity test passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["doc-parity"]}
